evals.name: Generate scenario from GPS + actor tracks using OpenStreetMap
evals.description: End-to-end test of the most common workflow: GPS + actor tracks + camera in -> OSM road download -> RoadRunner export -> simulation video -> side-by-side comparison. Tests actor-aware OSM buffer (Rule 11), fail-condition handling (Rule 4), preferred APIs, and dataset-folder video output (Rule 1).
evals.prompt: Generate a driving scenario from the data in C:/Users/adeshmuk/OneDrive - MathWorks/devai/sbskills/scenario_builder/scenario-builder-getting-started/VSILabs_Seq072. Use OpenStreetMap for roads. RoadRunner paths: rrAppPath='C:/Program Files/RoadRunner_R2025b_Update_2_win64/bin/win64', rrProjectPath='C:/Users/adeshmuk/CustomRRAssets/NewRoadRunnerProject'.

assertions.description: Inspects loaded data field names before accessing them (defensive against case-sensitivity issues)
assertions.expected: Uses Properties.VariableNames or fieldnames() before referencing fields

assertions.description: Computes actor-aware buffer from track lateral offsets before downloading OSM map (Rule 11)
assertions.expected: Computes max absolute lateral position from track data, e.g. max(abs(positions(:,2))), and uses it for the buffer

assertions.description: Calls getMapROI with Extent parameter for actor-aware bounding box
assertions.expected: getMapROI

assertions.description: Disables collision fail condition using setFailCondition with a DurationCondition (Rule 4)
assertions.expected: setFailCondition with a DurationCondition object so the simulation runs to completion despite expected collisions

assertions.description: Accesses RootPhase via PhaseLogic to set fail condition
assertions.expected: PhaseLogic.RootPhase

assertions.description: Simulates with EnableLogging=true to enable video export
assertions.expected: EnableLogging

assertions.description: Calls exportVideo after the simulation completes
assertions.expected: exportVideo

assertions.description: Creates a side-by-side comparison video (input vs simulation)
assertions.expected: Composes input recording and simulation video side-by-side using imresize and VideoWriter

assertions.description: Generates GPS vs Ego Trajectory visualization before exporting
assertions.expected: plot(gpsData, ...) and plot(egoTrajectory, ...) shown side-by-side via uipanel

assertions.description: Generates camera playback visualization (BEV+Camera or track-overlay) appropriate to whether camera intrinsics are present (Rule 2)
assertions.expected: plotBEVAndCamera or plotActorCircles or birdsEyePlot, chosen based on availability of CameraIntrinsics

assertions.description: Saves all videos in the dataset folder (dataDir), not in pwd (Rule 1)
assertions.expected: VideoWriter target path uses dataDir or fullfile(dataDir, ...), not fullfile(pwd, ...)

assertions.description: Does NOT use the legacy actorTracklist function (Rule 11)
assertions.expected: actorTracklist(

assertions.description: Does NOT use the deprecated roadrunnerLaneInfo function (Rule 11)
assertions.expected: roadrunnerLaneInfo(

assertions.description: Prints the saved video file path so the user knows where output landed (Rule 1)
assertions.expected: Saved:

assertions.description: Imports the OSM-derived HD Map into RoadRunner with overlap groups DISABLED (Rule 4, hard default)
assertions.expected: enableOverlapGroupsOptions(IsEnabled=false) wired into roadrunnerHDMapBuildOptions and roadrunnerHDMapImportOptions, then passed to importScene(..., "RoadRunner HD Map", ImportOptions=iOpts)

assertions.description: Uses correct property names on scenariobuilder objects (no guessed names from similar APIs)
assertions.expected: cameraData.Frames (NOT .Filenames / .Files / .ImageFileNames); trajectory.Position (NOT .Waypoints — Waypoints is the actorprops result-table column)

assertions.description: Emits ONE UI event per video — does not combine interactive play()/plotBEVAndCamera() with the save-video block
assertions.expected: For the visualization step, the script either (a) saves a video then shows ONE questdlg popup, or (b) calls play(...) interactively for ad-hoc inspection — never both for the same content

assertions.description: Every saved video ends with a questdlg+openFile popup (HARD RULE) — track-overlay, BEV, comparison, all
assertions.expected: After each close(vw) / exportVideo that produces a user-facing MP4, a questdlg 'Open it now? (Yes/No)' + openFile(path) block immediately follows. The script never just fprintf's the path and moves on.

assertions.description: Comparison video (input vs sim) burns timestamps on both halves using insertText
assertions.expected: insertText used to overlay 'Input t=X.Xs' on left half and 'Sim t=X.Xs' (or 'RoadRunner Sim t=X.Xs') on right half. Videos without timestamps fail this assertion.

assertions.description: Generated end-to-end script runs without error in MATLAB

evals.name: Generate scenario with ego localization (intrinsics available)
evals.description: Tests Workflow 8 (Lane Localization) when camera intrinsics are present. Agent must run lane detection with monoCamera, build laneData, fetch rrMap, run localizeEgoUsingLanes, verify localization improves ego-to-road distance, export both raw (red) and localized (green) trajectories, and use track-overlay video.
evals.prompt: Generate a driving scenario from the data in C:/Users/adeshmuk/OneDrive - MathWorks/devai/sbskills/scenario_builder/scenario-builder-getting-started/Pandaset_Seq90. Use OpenStreetMap for roads. RoadRunner paths: rrAppPath='C:/Program Files/RoadRunner_R2025b_Update_2_win64/bin/win64', rrProjectPath='C:/Users/adeshmuk/CustomRRAssets/NewRoadRunnerProject'.

assertions.description: Runs lane detection on cameraData (whose SensorParameters carries the monoCamera) and post-processes pixel coords through imageToVehicle + findParabolicLaneBoundaries before tracking
assertions.expected: detect(detector, cameraData, DetectionThreshold=..., OverlapThreshold=...) — cameraData built with SensorParameters=sensor — followed by per-frame imageToVehicle(sensor, validPts) and findParabolicLaneBoundaries before laneBoundaryTracker

assertions.description: Creates a laneData object from detections with timestamps and detected boundaries
assertions.expected: laneData

assertions.description: ASKS the user for startLaneIdx with ONE direct question — no lane-count plot, no summary table, no detection-based hint printed
assertions.expected: Script pauses to ask the user (e.g. via AskUserQuestion or chat prompt) for startLaneIdx (1 = leftmost driving lane, excluding shoulders). The question references the saved track-overlay video path. The script MUST NOT generate a lane_count_vs_frame.png plot, MUST NOT print a LaneCount/Frames/PercentOfRoute table, and MUST NOT print 'Detection-based HINT: ego in lane X of Y tracked boundaries'. The map lane-count gate runs silently (proximity loop on rrMap.Lanes); only surfaces if min(laneCount) < 1 or user's answer > min(laneCount).

assertions.description: Calls localizeEgoUsingLanes with rrMap (a roadrunnerHDMap object), NOT with an osmFile path
assertions.expected: localizeEgoUsingLanes(egoTrajectory, ..., rrMap, ...) — rrMap obtained from getRoadRunnerHDMap

assertions.description: Verifies localization with the user via the compare-then-ask gate — does NOT auto-reject based on median offset numbers
assertions.expected: Builds a side-by-side raw-camera-left + RR follow-cam-right video with red (raw) / green (localized) legends, opens it via questdlg, then asks the user in chat which trajectory to use. No median(offsetsLoc) vs median(offsetsRaw) auto-reject logic anywhere — that contradicts the compare-then-ask flow in workflow-08 Step 4.

assertions.description: Exports raw GPS trajectory in RED for visual comparison
assertions.expected: Color

assertions.description: Exports localized trajectory in GREEN for visual comparison
assertions.expected: Color or color set to green for the localized actor

assertions.description: Asks the user about localization quality before proceeding to delete the raw actor (Rule 1, step 6 ASK USER)
assertions.expected: Question to user about how the localization looks before continuing

assertions.description: Clears the red/green compare scenario via newScenario(rrApp) — does NOT call actor.delete() (unreliable) and does NOT relaunch RoadRunner
assertions.expected: After the user picks the localized trajectory, calls newScenario(rrApp) on the existing session, then re-exports only the chosen ego with Color="auto". Per-actor delete() API is unreliable and forbidden by workflow-08 Step 5.

assertions.description: Uses localizedTrajectory (NOT egoTrajectory) when calling actorprops for non-ego actors after localization
assertions.expected: actorprops(..., localizedTrajectory, ...)

assertions.description: Disables collision fail condition for the simulation
assertions.expected: setFailCondition

assertions.description: Accesses PhaseLogic.RootPhase to attach the fail condition
assertions.expected: PhaseLogic.RootPhase

assertions.description: Simulates with EnableLogging=true to enable video export
assertions.expected: EnableLogging

assertions.description: Exports simulation video using exportVideo
assertions.expected: exportVideo

assertions.description: Uses actor-aware OSM buffer via getMapROI Extent parameter
assertions.expected: getMapROI

assertions.description: Uses track-overlay video (plotActorCircles) when camera intrinsics are available (Rule 2 strict priority)
assertions.expected: plotActorCircles

assertions.description: Tries RVLD first for laneBoundaryDetector; falls back to CLRNet only if RVLD constructor errors (not installed).
assertions.expected: laneBoundaryDetector(Model="RVLD") in a try/catch with laneBoundaryDetector() (default CLRNet) as the fallback

assertions.description: Does NOT use the legacy actorTracklist function (Rule 11)
assertions.expected: actorTracklist(

assertions.description: Does NOT use the deprecated roadrunnerLaneInfo function (Rule 11)
assertions.expected: roadrunnerLaneInfo(

assertions.description: Imports the OSM-derived HD Map into RoadRunner with overlap groups DISABLED (hard default).
assertions.expected: enableOverlapGroupsOptions(IsEnabled=false) -> roadrunnerHDMapBuildOptions -> roadrunnerHDMapImportOptions, passed to importScene(..., "RoadRunner HD Map", ImportOptions=iOpts)

assertions.description: Uses correct property names on scenariobuilder objects (no guessed names from similar APIs)
assertions.expected: localizedTrajectory.Position / egoTrajectory.Position (NOT .Waypoints); cameraData.Frames (NOT .Filenames).

assertions.description: Builds and shows the raw-vs-localized compare video on EVERY pipeline run before asking the user which trajectory to use; never short-circuits on a prior session's choice or median-offset numbers.
assertions.expected: Script always builds the side-by-side compare (raw camera | RR follow with red/green legends), pops up a questdlg to open it, then asks the user in chat. No conditional skip based on cached preferences.

assertions.description: Every saved video (track-overlay, localization compare, input-vs-sim) ends with a questdlg+openFile popup — no exceptions
assertions.expected:
After each close(vw) or exportVideo that produces a user-facing MP4, the next non-comment MATLAB code is a questdlg block: questdlg(sprintf('... saved to:
%s

Open it now?', path), ..., 'Yes', 'No', 'Yes') with an if strcmp(ans,'Yes'), openFile(path); end branch. Videos are never just fprintf'd without the popup. Agent does NOT proceed to ask the next gate question (startLaneIdx, which trajectory) until confirming the popup script actually ran.

assertions.description: Does NOT generate a lane_count_vs_frame.png plot (misleading user-facing diagnostic)
assertions.expected: lane_count_vs_frame

assertions.description: Does NOT print a detection-based hint like 'ego in lane X of Y tracked boundaries' (Y is tracker count, not road lanes — misleads the user)
assertions.expected: Detection-based HINT

assertions.description: Generated end-to-end script runs without error in MATLAB

evals.name: Correct workflow ordering: validate sensors before export
evals.description: Tests that the agent follows the canonical step order from Rule 1's progress table: load -> visualize -> compute buffers -> download map -> build trajectory -> set fail conditions -> simulate -> export video -> create comparison. Out-of-order steps cause downstream failures.
evals.prompt: Generate a driving scenario from the data in C:/Users/adeshmuk/OneDrive - MathWorks/devai/sbskills/scenario_builder/scenario-builder-getting-started/VSILabs_Seq072. Use OpenStreetMap. RoadRunner paths: rrAppPath='C:/Program Files/RoadRunner_R2025b_Update_2_win64/bin/win64', rrProjectPath='C:/Users/adeshmuk/CustomRRAssets/NewRoadRunnerProject'.

assertions.description: GPS / trajectory visualization happens BEFORE the RoadRunner export
assertions.expected: plot(gpsData) and plot(egoTrajectory) appear earlier in the script than exportToRoadRunner

assertions.description: Camera validation (BEV+Camera or track-overlay) happens BEFORE the RoadRunner export
assertions.expected: plotBEVAndCamera, plotActorCircles, or birdsEyePlot appears earlier in the script than exportToRoadRunner

assertions.description: Actor-aware buffer calculation happens BEFORE getMapROI
assertions.expected: max abs lateral position computation appears earlier in the script than getMapROI

assertions.description: setFailCondition is called BEFORE simulateScenario
assertions.expected: setFailCondition appears earlier in the script than simulateScenario

assertions.description: simulateScenario(EnableLogging=true) runs BEFORE exportVideo
assertions.expected: simulateScenario appears earlier in the script than exportVideo

assertions.description: exportVideo runs BEFORE creating the side-by-side comparison video
assertions.expected: exportVideo appears earlier in the script than the comparison-video VideoWriter loop

assertions.description: BEV / overlay video save runs BEFORE comparison video creation (it's an input to the comparison)
assertions.expected: VideoWriter for BEV/overlay appears earlier in the script than the comparison video composition

assertions.description: Generated end-to-end script runs without error in MATLAB

evals.name: Localization API used correctly (common pitfalls)
evals.description: Tests the most common pitfalls when calling the localization APIs: passing osmFile string instead of rrMap, calling detect() without cameraParams, skipping the rrMap construction step, missing field-name inspection, simulation cleanup, and fail-condition duration tuning.
evals.prompt: Generate a driving scenario with ego localization from the data in C:/Users/adeshmuk/OneDrive - MathWorks/devai/sbskills/scenario_builder/scenario-builder-getting-started/VSILabs_Seq001. Use OpenStreetMap. RoadRunner paths: rrAppPath='C:/Program Files/RoadRunner_R2025b_Update_2_win64/bin/win64', rrProjectPath='C:/Users/adeshmuk/CustomRRAssets/NewRoadRunnerProject'.

assertions.description: NEVER passes an osmFile string directly to localizeEgoUsingLanes (it requires an rrMap object)
assertions.expected: localizeEgoUsingLanes(...,'.osm')

assertions.description: Builds cameraData with SensorParameters=monoCamera before detection, then post-processes pixel detections through imageToVehicle + findParabolicLaneBoundaries before laneBoundaryTracker
assertions.expected: scenariobuilder.CameraData(..., SensorParameters=sensor) where sensor=monoCamera(...). Detection chain is detect → imageToVehicle (with bounds filter) → findParabolicLaneBoundaries → laneBoundaryTracker → laneData. Skipping imageToVehicle / findParabolicLaneBoundaries is wrong.

assertions.description: Builds roadrunnerHDMap (rrMap) before calling localizeEgoUsingLanes
assertions.expected: getRoadRunnerHDMap

assertions.description: Inspects table variable names before accessing fields (defensive against case sensitivity)
assertions.expected: Properties.VariableNames or fieldnames() check

assertions.description: Deletes the stale simulation object (from SetupSimulation=true) via createSimulation+delete before calling simulateScenario, then acquires a fresh PhaseLogic.RootPhase AFTER all exports
assertions.expected: rrSim = createSimulation(rrApp); delete(rrSim); clear rrSim; — then roadrunnerAPI(rrApp).Scenario.PhaseLogic.RootPhase AFTER the last exportToRoadRunner call. Without the delete, simulateScenario errors with 'A RoadRunner Scenario Simulation object exists'.

assertions.description: Sets failCond.Duration longer than the trajectory duration so the sim runs to completion
assertions.expected: failCond.Duration set from egoTrajectory or localizedTrajectory duration plus a margin

assertions.description: Generated end-to-end script runs without error in MATLAB

evals.name: Add static objects to RoadRunner HD Map using roadrunnerStaticObjectInfo
evals.description: Tests Workflow 9 (Static Objects). Agent must use roadrunnerStaticObjectInfo (NOT manually construct roadrunner.hdmap.StaticObject), provide cuboids in the M×9 [xctr yctr zctr xlen ylen zlen xrot yrot zrot] format, organize them by category, assign back to rrMap, and write the HD map.
evals.prompt: Create a RoadRunner HD Map scene from the Pandaset_Seq90 GPS data with OpenStreetMap roads, and add 5 trees and 2 electric poles along the right side of the road. Use roadrunnerStaticObjectInfo. RoadRunner paths: rrAppPath='C:/Program Files/RoadRunner_R2025b_Update_2_win64/bin/win64', rrProjectPath='C:/Users/adeshmuk/CustomRRAssets/NewRoadRunnerProject'.

assertions.description: Uses roadrunnerStaticObjectInfo to convert cuboid detections into HD-map-ready static objects
assertions.expected: roadrunnerStaticObjectInfo

assertions.description: Organizes cuboids into a struct with category fields like trees, electricPoles, buildings, barricades, signs
assertions.expected: staticObjectCuboids.trees and/or .electricPoles fields

assertions.description: Provides the M×9 cuboid format [xctr yctr zctr xlen ylen zlen xrot yrot zrot]
assertions.expected: Each cuboid row contains 9 numeric values matching the documented format

assertions.description: Assigns staticObjectTypes back to rrMap.StaticObjectTypes
assertions.expected: StaticObjectTypes

assertions.description: Assigns staticObjects back to rrMap.StaticObjects
assertions.expected: StaticObjects

assertions.description: Writes the populated HD map to a .rrhd file
assertions.expected: write(rrMap, ...) to a .rrhd file path

assertions.description: Specifies AssetPath for realistic 3D rendering of the static objects
assertions.expected: AssetPath set in params for the relevant categories (trees, electricPoles, buildings, etc.)

assertions.description: NEVER manually constructs roadrunner.hdmap.StaticObject (use roadrunnerStaticObjectInfo instead)
assertions.expected: roadrunner.hdmap.StaticObject(

assertions.description: Generated script runs without error in MATLAB

evals.name: Final scenario on a flat OSM scene: actors must sit on road, distinct colors, lane-count probe
evals.description: Tests three failure modes that bit a real session: (1) all non-ego actors hard-coded to a single Color make individual behaviour unreadable in the sim video, (2) actor Waypoints(:,3) carries sensor-mount offset so on flat scenes (OSM-derived rrhd) every actor floats ~1 m above the road, (3) localizing on an OSM route with variable lane counts crashes localizeEgoUsingLanes mid-run when laneCount < startLaneIdx for some frames. Skill must use Color=auto, flatten Z on flat scenes, run the lane-count probe before localize, and surface variable-lane roads to the user with the crop-or-split choice — not silently push through.
evals.prompt: Generate a driving scenario from VSILabs_Seq072 (GPS + camera + actor tracks) using OpenStreetMap roads. The dataset is at //mathworks/WorldWide/IN-Hyderabad/devel/sandbox/adeshmuk/AI Agents/SPC Wave 3/Data/VSILabs_Seq072. Reuse camera intrinsics + height from VSILabs_Seq001 (same folder, parent path) since Seq072's sensorData.mat is missing them. Localize the ego if possible. RoadRunner paths: rrAppPath='C:/Program Files/RoadRunner_R2025b_Update_2_win64/bin/win64', rrProjectPath='C:/Users/adeshmuk/CustomRRAssets/NewRoadRunnerProject'.

assertions.description: Non-ego actors are exported with Color="auto" so RoadRunner assigns distinct colors per actor — NOT hard-coded to a single named color (yellow/blue/red/green).
assertions.expected: Every exportToRoadRunner call for a non-ego actor passes Color="auto". Hard-coded named colors are reserved for ego compare scenarios (raw vs localized, GPS_A vs GPS_B). Hard-coding all 11 actors to e.g. "yellow" is the failure mode this assertion guards against.

assertions.description: On flat OSM scenes, actor waypoint Z is flattened to 0 before constructing the scenariobuilder.Trajectory (otherwise the sensor-mount offset in actorInfo.Waypoints(:,3) makes every actor hover ~1 m above the road).
assertions.expected: In the actor export loop, the script reads actorInfo.Waypoints{i} into a local variable and sets wp(:,3) = 0 (or equivalent) before passing it to scenariobuilder.Trajectory(...). On flat scenes (OSM, OpenDRIVE without elevation, Pandaset) this is mandatory; on terrain-aware scenes (HERE HD, Zenrin, OpenDRIVE+CRG, point-cloud-derived) keep Z and rely on adjustHeight on ego instead.

assertions.description: Probes rrhd geometry Z range (and/or GPS altitude range) to decide whether the scene is flat before applying the Z-flatten — does not flatten unconditionally on terrain-aware scenes.
assertions.expected: Either an explicit comment/note that this OSM scene is flat (so Z-flatten is correct), or a min/max/range computation on the rrhd geometry Z (e.g. iterating LaneBoundaries / Lanes Geometry(:,3)). Hard-rule flattening on every scene is wrong — it would break HERE HD / OpenDRIVE-with-elevation.

assertions.description: Before calling localizeEgoUsingLanes, SILENTLY samples lane count along the ego path via a proximity loop on rrMap.Lanes — does NOT generate a user-facing plot or table, does NOT call findLanes (which does not exist on roadrunnerHDMap).
assertions.expected: Loop over ego waypoints using vecnorm(rrMap.Lanes(li).Geometry(:,1:2) - p, 2, 2) < radius to count lanes, populating a laneCount vector. The result is stored internally (min(laneCount) = maxValidStartLaneIdx) — NOT plotted, NOT printed as a table, NOT shown to the user. findLanes is NOT a valid method on roadrunnerHDMap.

assertions.description: When the route has variable lane counts (startLaneIdx > min(laneCount) for some frames), the script does NOT ask the user about it — it just calls localizeEgoUsingLanes with the user's startLaneIdx. The function interpolates through low-lane-count frames. Only report a problem if localizeEgoUsingLanes actually throws an error.
assertions.expected: After the user provides startLaneIdx, localizeEgoUsingLanes is called unconditionally — no crop/split question, no 'the map narrows to K lanes' message, no gate. If localizeEgoUsingLanes errors (index out of bounds or similar), THEN the script catches the error, reports the lane-count info, and offers crop/split options. Pre-emptive gating on variable lane counts is the failure mode this assertion guards against.

assertions.description: If localizeEgoUsingLanes throws due to lane-count mismatch, uses the canonical Trajectory.crop + laneData rebuild + updateTime pattern (laneData has no crop method).
assertions.expected: Only triggered by an actual error from localizeEgoUsingLanes (not pre-emptively). Calls crop(egoTrajectory, tStart, tEnd) on the trajectory, then rebuilds the laneData via laneData(timestamps, lbData, TrackIDs=ids) using the cropped frame range from .TimeStamp (singular) / .LaneBoundaryData / .TrackIDs, and re-zeros via normalizeTimestamps + .updateTime(.TimeStamp - tStart). Calling .crop() on a laneData object is wrong — it does not exist.

assertions.description: Imports the OSM-derived HD Map into RoadRunner with overlap groups DISABLED (hard default).
assertions.expected: enableOverlapGroupsOptions(IsEnabled=false) wired into roadrunnerHDMapBuildOptions and roadrunnerHDMapImportOptions, then passed to importScene(..., "RoadRunner HD Map", ImportOptions=iOpts).

assertions.description: Final-scenario camera mode is "front" on the ego (NOT follow). Follow is reserved for the localization compare.
assertions.expected: setCameraMode(rrApp, "front", FocusActorID=1) for the final scenario simulateScenario. setCameraMode(rrApp, "follow", ...) is acceptable only in the Step 4 raw-vs-localized compare.

assertions.description: Localization-compare export order: Ego_Localized exported BEFORE Ego_Raw / Ego_GPS_Raw, so FocusActorID=1 is the localized (green) ego. RoadRunner assigns ActorID in exportToRoadRunner call order; inverting it makes the follow-cam track the raw red trajectory.
assertions.expected: First exportToRoadRunner call in the localization compare is for localizedTrajectory (Name="Ego_Localized" or similar, Color="green"); the second is for egoTrajectory (raw GPS, Color="red"). The setCameraMode(rrApp, "follow", FocusActorID=1) call therefore tracks the localized ego.

assertions.description: Generated end-to-end script runs without error in MATLAB.

evals.name: Explicit DSD request: drivingScenario object with R2026a actor safeguards
evals.description: Tests Workflow 15 (Driving Scenario Designer). Triggered by an explicit 'Driving Scenario Designer' / drivingScenarioDesigner / drivingScenario request. Agent must build a drivingScenario object via exportToDrivingScenario, add non-ego actors with the four R2026a safeguards (dedup consecutive (x,y), finite-difference per-waypoint speed vector, 0.1 m/s clamp, trajectory() not smoothTrajectory), and finish with drivingScenarioDesigner(ds). RoadRunner APIs MUST NOT appear.
evals.prompt: Build a drivingScenario object from the data in C:/Users/adeshmuk/OneDrive - MathWorks/devai/sbskills/scenario_builder/scenario-builder-getting-started/Pandaset_Seq90 and open it in the Driving Scenario Designer app. Use OpenStreetMap for roads. Use the GPS_Corrected trajectory.

assertions.description: Calls exportToDrivingScenario with RoadNetworkSource="OpenStreetMap" to build the starting drivingScenario object
assertions.expected: exportToDrivingScenario

assertions.description: Hands the drivingScenario object off to the user via drivingScenarioDesigner(ds)
assertions.expected: drivingScenarioDesigner

assertions.description: Dedups consecutive identical (x,y) waypoints before adding the actor — drivingScenario rejects repeated waypoints with different speeds (R2026a safeguard 1).
assertions.expected: Per-actor preprocessing computes a keep mask such as [true; any(diff(wp(:,1:2),1,1) ~= 0, 2)] and uses it to subset waypoints/time/yaw before calling trajectory(veh, ...).

assertions.description: Builds a non-empty per-waypoint speed vector from finite differences before calling trajectory() — R2026a rejects [] / scalar speed (safeguard 2).
assertions.expected: Per-actor speed computed as dxy = vecnorm(diff(wpu(:,1:2),1,1), 2, 2); dt = max(diff(tu), 1e-3); spdu = [dxy./dt; dxy(end)/dt(end)]; passed positionally to trajectory(veh, wpu, spdu).

assertions.description: Clamps the speed vector to a small positive floor (e.g. 0.1 m/s) — exact-zero segment speeds are rejected or stall DSD playback (safeguard 3).
assertions.expected: spdu = max(spdu, 0.1) (or equivalent floor between 0.05 and 0.5 m/s) applied before trajectory(veh, wpu, spdu).

assertions.description: Does NOT use smoothTrajectory on recorded actor waypoints — its accumulateDistance is brittle on sparse / near-stationary / noisy paths (safeguard 4).
assertions.expected: smoothTrajectory(

assertions.description: Flattens actor waypoint Z to 0 before adding to the drivingScenario — actorInfo.Waypoints(:,3) is sensor-mount offset, not road height; drivingScenario uses 2D ground-plane waypoints.
assertions.expected: wp(:,3) = 0 (or equivalent) applied to the per-actor Waypoints matrix before constructing/adding the actor trajectory.

assertions.description: Does NOT call any RoadRunner API — Workflow 15 is end-to-end DSD, not a RoadRunner export.
assertions.expected: exportToRoadRunner

assertions.description: Does NOT launch RoadRunner via roadrunner(...) for a DSD-only request.
assertions.expected: roadrunner(

assertions.description: Generated end-to-end script runs without error in MATLAB.

evals.name: Generic 'driving scenario' phrasing must default to RoadRunner, not DSD
evals.description: Negative-direction guard for Workflow 15. The phrase 'driving scenario' (lowercase, generic English) means 'a scenario built from driving data/logs' — that is a RoadRunner request. Workflow 15 only fires on explicit code-form / DSD triggers (drivingScenario class, drivingScenarioDesigner, 'DSD', 'Driving Scenario Designer'). The agent must pick RoadRunner here and must NOT open Driving Scenario Designer or use the drivingScenarioDesigner / standalone-drivingScenario hand-off.
evals.prompt: Build a driving scenario from the recorded data in C:/Users/adeshmuk/OneDrive - MathWorks/devai/sbskills/scenario_builder/scenario-builder-getting-started/Pandaset_Seq90. Use OpenStreetMap for roads. RoadRunner paths: rrAppPath='C:/Program Files/RoadRunner_R2025b_Update_2_win64/bin/win64', rrProjectPath='C:/Users/adeshmuk/CustomRRAssets/NewRoadRunnerProject'.

assertions.description: Routes through the RoadRunner workflow — calls roadrunner(rrProjectPath, ...) to launch the app.
assertions.expected: roadrunner(

assertions.description: Builds the RoadRunner HD Map via getRoadRunnerHDMap (Rule 4 step 4).
assertions.expected: getRoadRunnerHDMap

assertions.description: Exports to RoadRunner via exportToRoadRunner — the canonical Scenario Builder target.
assertions.expected: exportToRoadRunner

assertions.description: Does NOT hand the user off to Driving Scenario Designer — the request never named DSD or the drivingScenario class.
assertions.expected: drivingScenarioDesigner(

assertions.description: Any exportToDrivingScenario call (if present) is intermediate (consumed by getRoadRunnerHDMap), not a final hand-off — i.e., the resulting scenario object is not saved as the deliverable and is not opened in DSD.
assertions.expected: If exportToDrivingScenario appears, its return value feeds getRoadRunnerHDMap or is discarded. The final artifact is a RoadRunner scene/scenario, not a saved drivingScenario .mat handed back to the user.

assertions.description: Imports the OSM-derived HD Map into RoadRunner with overlap groups DISABLED (Rule 4 hard default).
assertions.expected: enableOverlapGroupsOptions(IsEnabled=false) wired into roadrunnerHDMapBuildOptions and roadrunnerHDMapImportOptions, then passed to importScene(..., "RoadRunner HD Map", ImportOptions=iOpts).

assertions.description: Generated end-to-end script runs without error in MATLAB.

evals.name: Pre-built scene + Corrected GPS: localization must be asked, not auto-run
evals.description: Tests the 'localization is optional' rule. When the user provides a pre-built .rrscene (HERE HD / vendor) AND chooses Corrected GPS, the agent must ASK whether to localize before running lane detection + localizeEgoUsingLanes. Auto-running localization on top of a trusted GPS wastes time and can degrade the trajectory if the map geometry doesn't perfectly match. Also tests: worldSettings(rrApp).WorldOrigin is 1x2 (must append 0), adjustHeight is called on terrain-aware scenes, and the no-variable-lane-gate rule.
evals.prompt: Digitize this driving data: C:/Users/adeshmuk/OneDrive - MathWorks/devai/data/Polysync_Seq_05_hm. It has a pre-built HERE HD scene (.rrscene) and both Raw and Corrected GPS. Use Corrected GPS. RoadRunner paths: rrAppPath='C:/Program Files/RoadRunner_R2025b_Update_2_win64/bin/win64', rrProjectPath='C:/Users/adeshmuk/CustomRRAssets/NewRoadRunnerProject'.

assertions.description: ASKs the user whether to apply lane localization or use the Corrected GPS trajectory directly — does NOT auto-run localization.
assertions.expected: After building the ego trajectory and loading the scene, the agent asks a question like 'Do you want to apply lane localization (snaps trajectory to lane center), or use the corrected GPS trajectory directly?' BEFORE running any lane detection. The agent does NOT silently proceed to detect + localizeEgoUsingLanes.

assertions.description: Opens the pre-built .rrscene via openScene(rrApp, path), does NOT use getRoadRunnerHDMap (which is for OSM-derived roads only).
assertions.expected: openScene(rrApp, projectScenePath) where the .rrscene has been copied into the RR project's Scenes/ folder. getRoadRunnerHDMap does NOT appear in the pre-built-scene path.

assertions.description: Extracts LocalOrigin from worldSettings(rrApp).WorldOrigin and appends 0 altitude if it returns 1x2.
assertions.expected: ws = worldSettings(rrApp); localOrigin = ws.WorldOrigin; if numel(localOrigin)==2, localOrigin = [localOrigin, 0]; end — then passes localOrigin to trajectory(gpsData, 'LocalOrigin', localOrigin).

assertions.description: Exports scene to .rrhd and reads into roadrunnerHDMap via the 2-step pattern (NOT positional constructor).
assertions.expected: exportScene(rrApp, rrhdFile, 'RoadRunner HD Map') then rrMap = roadrunnerHDMap; read(rrMap, rrhdFile). NOT roadrunnerHDMap(rrhdFile) positionally.

assertions.description: Calls adjustHeight(egoTrajectory, rrMap) on the terrain-aware HERE HD scene.
assertions.expected: adjustHeight(egoTrajectory, rrMap) appears after trajectory is built with the scene's LocalOrigin.

assertions.description: If user says 'localize', does NOT ask about variable lane counts — just runs localizeEgoUsingLanes with the user's startLaneIdx.
assertions.expected: After startLaneIdx is provided, localizeEgoUsingLanes is called directly. No 'the map narrows to K lanes through part of the route' question, no crop/split offer. Only reports an issue if localizeEgoUsingLanes actually throws an error.

assertions.description: If user says 'use trajectory as-is', skips lane detection, tracking, and localization entirely — goes straight to scenario export.
assertions.expected: When the user declines localization, the script skips laneBoundaryDetector, laneBoundaryTracker, laneData, and localizeEgoUsingLanes. Proceeds directly to exportToRoadRunner with the corrected GPS trajectory.

assertions.description: Every saved video ends with a questdlg+openFile popup (HARD RULE).
assertions.expected: After each close(vw) or exportVideo, a questdlg 'Open it now?' + openFile(path) block follows.

assertions.description: Generated end-to-end script runs without error in MATLAB.
